rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Injunctive Order And For Appointment Of A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 10, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE INJUNCTIVE ORDER AND FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER is OFF CALENDAR for Department 501. Parties to contact the trial department, Department 220, to obtain a hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHANTEL MCCLENDON ET AL VS. THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Or, Alternatively, To Strike The Protected Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 10, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO STRIKE THE PROTECTED PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to strike the entire First Amended Complaint for Damages is DENIED. Motion is GRANTED as to each claim to the extent it arises out of protected activity of serving notice of termination of tenancy any prosecuting unlawful detainer actions. Motion is otherwise DENIED. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.=(501/CFH)
